Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. My first patch to the Linux kernel 153 upvotes, 27 comments
  2. Electronics for Kids, 2nd Edition 227 upvotes, 47 comments
  3. Bored of eating your own dogfood? Try smelling your own farts 90 upvotes, 19 comments
  4. Some things just take time 766 upvotes, 246 comments
  5. A Review of Dice That Came with the White Castle 29 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Why Lab Coats Turned White 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 36 upvotes, 6 comments
  8. Floci – A free, open-source local AWS emulator 231 upvotes, 72 comments
  9. Professional video editing, right in the browser with WebGPU and WASM 315 upvotes, 112 comments
  10. HopTab–free,open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 67 upvotes, 19 comments
  11. 25 Years of Eggs 124 upvotes, 42 comments
  12. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Nintendo's not-AI, not-a-game toy 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. Flash-Moe: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Mac with 48GB RAM 132 upvotes, 46 comments
  15. Turns out your coffee addiction may be doing your brain a favor 21 upvotes, 4 comments
  16. Tinybox – A powerful computer for deep learning 534 upvotes, 305 comments
  17. 'Miracle': Europe reconnects with lost spacecraft 64 upvotes, 27 comments
  18. The three pillars of JavaScript bloat 388 upvotes, 227 comments
  19. Hormuz Minesweeper – Are you tired of winning? 442 upvotes, 266 comments
  20. Chest Fridge (2009) 146 upvotes, 78 comments
  21. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 27 upvotes, 10 comments
  22. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 32 upvotes, 13 comments
  23. Brute-Forcing My Algorithmic Ignorance with an LLM in 7 Days 20 upvotes, 7 comments
  24. Vatican Rebukes Peter Thiel's Antichrist Lectures in Rome 89 upvotes, 53 comments
  25. A Case Against Currying 24 upvotes, 19 comments
  26. Show HN: Crack – Turn your MacBook into a squeaky door 8 upvotes, 4 comments
  27. Revise – An AI Editor for Documents 13 upvotes, 9 comments
  28. How We Synchronized Editing for Rec Room's Multiplayer Scripting System 9 upvotes, 6 comments
  29. Convincing Is Not Persuading 7 upvotes, 5 comments
  30. $ teebot.dev – from terminal to tee in 6 seconds 9 upvotes, 9 comments

Last generated 10:30pm, 22 March 2026. Source here.
